problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Synaptic plasticity modulation via NMDA receptor
hypothesis: The key to Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? is Synaptic plasticity modulation via NMDA receptor — specifically Synaptic plasticity modulation — which would help 12,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: EEG/MEG signal analysis with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance. — Confucius
